There's technical things, lot of times we have to share. So for me, what I do, every time I'm explaining anything, you probably noticed it through the last day and a half, I'll go into something and I'll be telling the story. Also, I hit something I have to share because it's part of the thing, right? So like when I did the pitch for Prove It, I say like, da-da-da-da, key tones. And as soon as I hit that word key tones, I know that everyone's like, boom, technical babble and everyone like instantly is just like, in the zone out. So every time I'm telling a story, I hit a technical thing, I stop right there and I do what I call kind of like bridge. I say, it's kind of like a million motivational speakers running through your body, giving you insane amounts of energy. And they're like, oh yeah, that's pretty, like, that's how I do it. So every time I hit any kind of concept that I think people are going to be stuck on, I stop and I'm like, it's kind of like, boom, that's like my bridge. Every time I hit something. So I'm telling the story, there's going to be technical things in this journey. Every time I hit that, I stop and I do a kind of like bridge. It's just really quick thing like, new concept, what do they already understand? The new concept, what is it something that it's kind of like that they can already understand? And as soon as you bridge that, they're like, oh, this makes perfect sense. Thank you so much. And that's the key. Like they're just, they're super fast. And it's, every time you have a technical babble, it has to slip out for them to understand a piece. The second slips out, I switch, boom, it's kind of like this. And then, and then they understand. Kind of like bridges are the coolest.
